zh.lifeSummary: 當Joseph Child於1696年1月7日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利時，他的父親Joshua Child39歲，母親Elizabeth Morris30歲。他於1722年11月29日與Abigail Bridges在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1765年9月27日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州文達哈姆武德斯托可享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joseph Child于1696年1月7日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利时，他的父亲Joshua Child39岁，母亲Elizabeth Morris30岁。他于1722年11月29日与Abigail Bridges在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1765年9月27日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州文達哈姆武德斯托可享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: Child
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname from Middle English child ‘child, infant’ (Old English cild), in various possible applications. The word is found in Old English as a byname, and in Middle English as a widely used affectionate term of address. It was also used as a term of status for a young man of noble birth, although the exact meaning is not clear; in the 13th and 14th centuries it was a technical term used of a young noble awaiting elevation to the knighthood. In other cases it may have been applied as a byname to a youth considerably younger than his brothers or to one who was a minor on the death of his father.
in Kent, possibly a topographic name from Old English cielde ‘spring (water)’, a rare word derived from c(e)ald ‘cold’.

variants.name: Barnes
meanings: English: habitational name from Barnes (on the Surrey bank of the Thames in London), named with Old English bere-ærn ‘barn, a storehouse for barley and other grain’, or a topographic name or metonymic occupational name for someone who lived by or worked at a barn or barns, from Middle English barn ‘barn, granary’.

English: variant of Barne, with excrescent -s, derived from either the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn (based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn or Old English Beorn, both from a word meaning ‘warrior’), or from Middle English barn (Old Norse barn) ‘child’. The latter term is found as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child (see Child ).
Irish: in Ireland in many cases this is no doubt the English name, but in others it is possibly an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Bearáin ‘descendant of Bearán’, a byname meaning ‘spear’.

variants.name: Chill
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Derbyshire): variant of   Child   or   Chell  .

variants.name: Childs
meanings: English: variant of Child , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Chilton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Chilton, for example in Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, County Durham, Hampshire, Kent, Shropshire, Somerset, Suffolk, and Wiltshire. The majority are shown by early forms to derive from Old English cild ‘child’ (see Child ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. One place of this name in Somerset possibly gets its first element from Old English cealc ‘chalk, limestone’, and one on the Isle of Wight from the personal name Cēola (compare Chilcott ), or from Old English ceole ‘deep valley’.

variants.name: Bambino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmine, Angelo.

Italian: from Bambino, a byname meaning ‘child’ (see Child ), or a personal name given with reference to Gesù Bambino ‘the Christ child’, the use of Gesù as a personal name being considered taboo in Italy.

variants.name: Chilson
meanings: English: habitational name from Chilson in Oxfordshire, named with Old English cild ‘young man’ (see Child ) + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’.

History: It is not known when this surname was first brought to America, but it was already established in CT in the early 18th century. Daniel Chilson of Weathersfield, CT, was born c. 1720 and on 4 October 1745 married Sybil Stanclift in Middlesex County, CT.

variants.name: Fairchild
meanings: English (southern): nickname from Middle English fair child ‘handsome child’ or ‘handsome youth of noble birth’ (see Child ).

variants.name: Childers
meanings: English: probably a habitational name from some lost place called Childerhouse, from Old English cildra, genitive plural of cild ‘child’ + hūs ‘house’. This may have referred to some form of orphanage perhaps run by a religious order, or perhaps the first element is to be understood in its later sense as a term of status (see Child ).

nameMeanings.name: Joseph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the biblical Hebrew name Yosef, meaning ‘(God) shall add (another son)’. This was borne by the favourite son of Jacob, whose brothers became jealous of him and sold him into slavery (Genesis 37). He was taken to Egypt, where he rose to become chief steward to Pharaoh, and was eventually reconciled to his brothers when they came to buy corn during a seven-year famine (Genesis 43–7). In the New Testament Joseph is the name of the husband of the Virgin Mary. It is also borne by a rich Jew, Joseph of Arimathea (Matthew 27:57; Mark 15:43; Luke 23:50; John 19:38), who took Jesus down from the Cross, wrapped him in a shroud, and buried him in a rock tomb. According to medieval legend, Joseph of Arimathea brought the Holy Grail to Britain. The name was uncommon in Britain in the Middle Ages but was revived in the mid 16th century and had become popular by the 1630s, remaining so ever since.

variants.name: Josef
meanings: , Dutch, Scandinavian, and Czech spelling of   Joseph  , now also in use in the English‐speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Joseph Child was born on 7 January 1696, in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, his father, Joshua Child, was 39 and his mother, Elizabeth Morris, was 30. He married Abigail Bridges on 29 November 1722, in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 27 September 1765, in Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joseph Child est né le 7 janvier 1696, à Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Joshua Child, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Morris, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Abigail Bridges le 29 novembre 1722, à Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 27 septembre 1765, à Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 69 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joseph Child am 7. Januar 1696 in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater Joshua Child 39 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Morris 30. Er hat am 29. November 1722 in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America Abigail Bridges geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 27. September 1765 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joseph Child è nato il il 7 gennaio 1696 nel Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, il suo padre, Joshua Child, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Morris, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Abigail Bridges il 29 novembre 1722, nel Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 27 settembre 1765, nel Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 69 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joseph Childが1696年1月7日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリーで生まれた時，父Joshua Childは39歳，母Elizabeth Morrisは30歳であった。1722年11月29日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリーでAbigail Bridgesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1765年9月27日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ウィンドハムウッドストックで69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joseph Child(이)가 1696년 1월 7일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Joshua Child(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Morris(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1722년 11월 29일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이에서 Abigail Bridges(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1765년 9월 27일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 윈드함 우드스톡에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joseph Child nasceu em 7 de janeiro de 1696, em Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, Joshua Child, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Morris, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Abigail Bridges em 29 de novembro de 1722, em Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 27 de setembro de 1765, em Woodstock, Windham, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 69 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joseph Child родился 7 января 1696 г. в местности роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Joshua Child было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Morris, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Abigail Bridges 29 ноября 1722 г. в местности роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер 27 сентября 1765 г. в местности вудстокк, виндхам, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 69 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joseph Child nació el 7 de enero de 1696, en Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, Joshua Child, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Morris, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Abigail Bridges el 29 de noviembre de 1722, en Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 27 de septiembre de 1765, en Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 69 años.
